daily.bias_reason: Nifty 50 closed Jun 22 at 24,102.9 (+89.8 pts, +0.37%), IT sector staged a sharp reversal (+0.74% after Friday's -6.5% carnage) while Bank Nifty added +0.43%; broader Nifty 500 breadth strengthened to 65% (325A/175D) though Nifty 50 breadth remained narrow at 54% (27A/23D), and VIX fell to 12.84 (-3.88%), its lowest close in weeks. FII provisional data for Jun 22 registered -₹635.91 Cr — a fourth consecutive net-sell session but the mildest, offset by DII buying of +₹1,035.72 Cr; the T1 Capitulation Reversal trigger (3 consecutive FII buy days) remains unactivated, keeping conviction capped despite an improving MMI now at 68.3 (Greed).
daily.watch: Watch for first FII provisional net-buy print on Jun 23 — any positive flow would be the first since the exodus began; also watch 24,200 as the next resistance (Jun 19 intraday high), and Brent crude near $77.91 after a -1.33% slide (WTI -2.63%) which could weigh on NIFTY Oil & Gas but supports INR and RBI easing expectations.

nifty.note: Jun 22 close. NSE official: 24,102.9 (+89.8 pts). Jun 23 market not yet open at scan time (8 AM IST).

vix.note: Jun 22 close. Down from 12.97 prev. VIX < 13 = low fear, bullish signal. Yahoo v7 shows vs Jun 19: -3.88%.

usdinr.note: NSE currency futures live at 8 AM IST Jun 23. Yahoo v7: 94.67 (prev 94.89). INR mildly weaker vs prior day.

pcr.note: NSE option chain endpoint returned 403. PCR unavailable for 5th consecutive session.

breadth.note: Nifty500 breadth crossed bullish 65% threshold. Nifty50 breadth 54% = cautious. Total market: 475A/274D = 63%.

fii.note: Jun 22 provisional: FII net -₹635.91 Cr (4th consecutive sell day, mildest). DII: +₹1,035.72 Cr (net buyer). 7d FII trend: negative streak continues. T1 setup not triggered.

crude.note: Brent Jun 23 live (Yahoo v7). WTI: $74.05 (-2.63%). Crude softening supports INR, RBI easing thesis. NIFTY Oil & Gas +0.92% on Jun 22 despite softer crude (sector lagging move).

mmi.note: Tickertape MMI Jun 22 14:20 UTC = 68.33. Jun 19: 67.74. Trend: steadily rising from 55.8 (Jun 16). CNN F&G proxy: 34.74 (Fear) — divergence noted, India MMI and global F&G diverging.

weekly.summary: Nifty reclaimed 24,000 this week, closing at 24,013 (+1.65%) after tagging an intraweek high of 24,189 on Thursday, then fading into Friday alongside Bank Nifty (57,686, also red on Friday). The real driver was intermarket, not flows: WTI crude collapsed ~10% to $76.5 (Brent $80.6) and USDINR firmed to ~94.3 even as the dollar index pushed to a three-week high near 100.85. That INR-up-while-DXY-up divergence is the week's most important tell — it implies crude relief and genuine inflow are absorbing dollar strength, quietly undercutting the FII-exodus narrative. The non-obvious risk: this entire reclaim happened with zero confirmed FII buy data, so the marginal buyer is unverified — likely DII SIPs plus short-covering. If DXY keeps climbing past 101, the crude tailwind gets overwhelmed and FII selling can resume into resistance. Friday's twin fade after a five-day run argues the easy money is made. Posture: constructive but unconfirmed — the macro backdrop (cheap crude, firm INR) is the best in weeks, but conviction stays capped until FII flow prints positive.
weekly.market_posture: Constructive on macro, unconfirmed on flows — hold 24k, but await FII buy-print before adding longs into resistance.

threads.name: FII Exodus — Structural Outflow
threads.pov: Intermarket evidence shifted against the bear thesis this week: INR strengthened to ~94.3 despite a rising dollar, and the index reclaimed 24k — behavior inconsistent with active heavy selling. But no confirmed FII provisional buy data printed all week, so the outflow cannot be declared over; upgrading red to yellow on improving conditions, not resolution.
threads.watch_next_week: First confirmed FII provisional cash print Monday. 3 consecutive net-buy days activates the T1 Capitulation Reversal setup; a single >₹5,000 Cr sell day flips it back to red.

threads.name: RBI Rate Cycle — Transmission Lag
threads.pov: The ~10% crude crash and a firming rupee materially ease the inflation/INR constraint on RBI, giving the easing path more room without defending the currency. Thesis intact and incrementally more favorable; banks led most of the week (Bank Nifty +1.5%) before Friday's fade.
threads.watch_next_week: Any RBI commentary on INR vs. growth; banking/NBFC follow-through after Friday's pullback; whether cheaper crude shows up in inflation expectations.

threads.name: Monsoon 2026 Onset
threads.pov: No fresh IMD weekly data was retrievable this week, leaving onset progress unconfirmed. Thesis unchanged: normal/above-normal rainfall validates the rural-consumption recovery; any deficit caps consumer-name upside. Holding yellow pending data.
threads.watch_next_week: IMD weekly rainfall vs. long-period average and core-monsoon-zone coverage; FMCG/two-wheeler/agri-input price action as a market proxy.

weekly_povs.summary: Dashboard initialized. First weekly synthesis will run Saturday June 14, 2026.
weekly_povs.market_posture: Cautious. FII selling persistent. Hold reduced leverage until FII flow turns or RBI/monsoon provides positive catalyst.

threads.name: FII Exodus — Structural Outflow
threads.pov: FII sold ₹76,408 Cr in 30 days — likely driven by DXY strength and EM rotation to China. DII SIP flows are the floor. T1 Capitulation Reversal setup forming. When FII turns, reversal will be sharp.
threads.watch_next_week: Daily FII cash — 3 consecutive buy days = T1 model activates. Monitor FII L/S ratio on NSE Bhavcopy.

threads.name: RBI Rate Cycle — Transmission Lag
threads.pov: RBI cuts are in system (repo at 5.75%) but credit growth and housing recovery are lagging. June MPC outcome + INR trajectory will determine if easing continues or pauses.
threads.watch_next_week: June MPC decision and RBI governor language on INR vs. growth trade-off. Banking/NBFC stocks reaction.
sources: RBI policy history, backtest W01 Dec 2025

threads.name: Monsoon 2026 Onset
threads.pov: June onset month. Normal/above-normal monsoon validates rural consumption recovery (FMCG, two-wheelers, agri). Deficit extends rural squeeze and caps Nifty upside in consumer names.
threads.watch_next_week: IMD forecast update. Kerala/Karnataka onset timing. Rainfall vs. long-period average.
sources: IMD seasonal outlook, seasonal pattern analysis
